comment.body:
I personally can relate to both sides. These markets are challenging to navigate and without the right process and experience these markets will drain your wallet and you emotionally. I agree that some of the questions are very basic and should better be asked to google or ChatGPT than this forum.  But since you, Alma, are promoting this sub very actively on twitter, this has to be accepted and is just a testament of your popularity and appeal to all kinds of investors/traders. So I think they should just be accepted and/or ignored.
I personally also agree that the substack has lost some of its (still extraordinary value) for me, due to the larger focus on geopolitics. I am conducting my own analysis and doing my own research. I am here to refine my edge. That’s why I don’t even check the trades because I don’t care about the signals. But since I’m here to become even better, that implies by definition adapting or overruling some of my own systems/processes when I think the current times might be not accurately reflected in my historical dataset/backtest. So I ended up overrruling my own systems to some extent because I placed higher value on some of your statements in the beginning of April than on my own process. This discretionary decision lead to some losses on my side and missing some of the upside. I don’t blame you, for that because it is my own responsibility. But I get that people might be frustrated, even the ones that are not just waiting for up or down advice. Because exactly the fact that I did not follow your trades but took your context analysis as a factor in my decision making lead for me personally to a worse situation as if I would have just blindly followed your trading.
My problem with all the macro stuff is that it is by definition irrelevant to my process (I am aware about the contradiction…) as long as it is based on insider sources that I cannot access. I don’t know who these insiders are, what is their motivation to share with you and what exactly they share with you. I don’t know if the information is accurate and I don’t know if your conclusion about the information shared is logical. This is not meant as criticism but just as my approach to assessing the information you provide. It did not help that some of the information you seem to have received from your (Russian) insider read like straight propaganda or in fact was propaganda that could have easily fact checked even then (for example the allegedly 1000 encircled Ukrainian soldiers in Kursk). So because of these facts I personally discount a lot of this information. That being said a lot of your calls were accurate,so I am not discrediting per se. But it exemplifies the challenge to filter the signals from the noise. The same applies to some extent to your market calls(short vol etc.). It introduces a bias without telling on what analysis/framework/indicator that bias is based on. For somebody eager to learn and improve that makes it hard to use these because I either can blindly follow (which I don’t want to) or just ignore this information because I have no way of reproducing it systematically. That’s why I think your current impulse to prove your trading prowess or the accuracy of your calls is also misguided because it contradicts your own stated premise for this substack of people thinking for themselves and learning how to trade under your guidance. Blindly following your trades is the opposite of that, even if they where absolutely spot on.
And as has been said before, once you leave (if you still leave), none of this will stay with us and therefore is hardly relevant for people who are building long term systems to trade and invest in the markets. For this to be the case, a lot more of the framework would need to be laid out and the line separating own deduction from inside info would need to be even clearer. And even if we leave the geopolitics out and just focus on the economic stuff in your daily analysis it is sometimes hard to understand why you show certain things on a specific day. Your daily and weekly analysis reads much more professional in tone and refined in grammar/spelling than in the beginning but sometimes it feels like some numbers/charts are just included to fill the blank because the relevance to the given day or the general trading environment is sometimes not obvious.
One thing I am personally disappointed about is that there is no engagement from your side with the more technical/coding related questions. Me and others have asked questions about model parametrizations/implementations but rarely have received an answer.
But after all the criticism I want to end this on a positive note. You provide an incredible amount of value and I am still a happy subscriber. I am thankful for what you do and the service you provide to us. I can hardly fathom the pressure you must feel to perform every day and to try to navigate these markets for thousands of readers. So please take my criticism under that premise.

comment.body:
I honestly don’t even know what to say at this point - but rather than focusing on public recognition on Twitter, I believe this might be a good moment to reflect on how communication with your subscribers could be improved.
Since early April, with the growing emphasis on geo-narratives, it feels like the sub has lost some of the clarity and practical value it previously offered. I genuinely feel for those who fully embraced the more extreme bearish views and ended up taking significant losses - several of them have shared their experiences openly in the chat.
It’s also unfortunate that people who struggle to interpret your posts are sometimes dismissed as inexperienced or naive. From my perspective, the intraday and weekly updates in recent weeks have become increasingly difficult to follow. The messaging often points in multiple directions and is later referenced with statements like “as I said….” Regardless of how we frame it, this sub either missed a sharp "bear market rally" - or a very effective "bull trap".
There’s been a consistent drumbeat around “risk/reward favors the short side,” with repeated downside targets like 4,400 or even 3,996. Phrases such as “we are not out of the woods”, "upcoming earnings (TSLA, GOOG this week) will be weak" have become routine. While I absolutely respect your analyses and your talent as a trader, I personally find that so-called “high conviction” trades - often accompanied by disclaimers like “very small allocation” or “big risk” - don’t offer the directional clarity I’m looking for, especially as someone who doesn’t trade short-term timeframes. And as you know, negative narratives tend to resonate more deeply - and linger longer - than positive ones.
Your recent comment - “The goal is to have money for your daily bread, not to be right on the market direction” - made it clear that directional accuracy may not be your primary objective. Looking back, the actual market behavior over the past month diverged significantly from what many of your analyses suggested (I think this will not be highlighted on twitter?). And now we’re seeing retrospective explanations that the market broke through key inflection points because of government interventions or a so-called “Trump Put.” As I understood - the market also fell in the past due to government actions. And if I interpreted it correctly, that “Trump Put” was supposedly on April 9?
Until the end of March, the learning curve here was steep but extremely valuable. The analysis was sharp, neutral, and grounded in positioning and structure - and I truly appreciated the insights I could apply to my own trading. Posts like the one yesterday on volatility and momentum are genuinely educational, and that’s the kind of value I’m here for.
Since the beginning of April, however, we’ve increasingly leaned into speculative geo-narratives and potential insider information - even while multiple indicators were flashing extreme conditions. I fully agree that the broader macro and geopolitical picture matters and should be considered. But earlier this year, the sub felt more balanced, with a focus on market internals, sentiment, and data. That balance now feels lost or I don't see it. The current tone is much more doomsday-driven, and at times it feels like we’re just waiting for the next bad headline to confirm an already bearish bias. Will we now remain that tone in this sub for the next 3 years until the "real bottom" is called?

Maybe I’ll catch some backlash for sharing this view, and maybe I just “misinterpreted” things. But I still think it’s worth pointing out that failed calls are rarely acknowledged - while the wins are amplified, particularly by the louder voices in the community who always seem to have “read it right” in hindsight.

Bottom line is, and I know this’ll un-cuddle some ppl here, if you’re sitting around every morning all day waiting for a signal or call from Alma, you’re doing it wrong. Alma isn’t God. I don’t know who has that kind of free time anyways, but that isn’t a successful mindset. I love Alma’s posts and value them immensely, but I sometimes get busy and won’t read her Posts, signals, or chats in a couple days—yet I’m still making $. Her posts are a tool, not a cure.  Her signals, should be looked at like you read footnotes. 
If you’re waiting for signals or crying about bad calls, take that time to read or tweak your own strat before trade/following. Or try a paper account lol, idk. But don’t sit here crying/shit-posting about how her calls are bad when a ton of people are getting it and making $$. 
It’s you, not her.

comment.body:
you know... 
i didn't know that this will be this fucking hard. 
I mean, mentally. Bcs there is a lot of things to process and interpret. 
On twitter, I only posted the final conclusions (Terrance), but here I wanted to show you the connections.
I know that these geo breakdown were simply to much for ppl, especially for those who are not familiar with the ideologies, and these processes. But my goal was to show how I arrive to a certain conclusions. 
The "voting machine/weighing machine" thing. Last August or Dec was the voting machine... this was the weighing machine.  
And what I wanted to show you is what the market is trying to value. The intraday posts, lvls, charts were still there along with my momentum script. 

The key is that when the "weighing machine" turns on, the regime shifts. The market restructures itself, and it doesn't seem like a 2022-type of bear market looming, but more like a 70s-80s like or a dot-com like, as some analysts also keep pointing this up.
A quant needs to understand this too.
In some of my educational posts I dropped that sentiment is also monitored, very closely by quant desks. Ppl don't like to talk about it, but it is being done. 
And this is why I thought to share the processes, describing the intention behind US, and its POV (not taking any party personally), bcs that will dictate the decisions. And events are unfolding. 

But, as I said, and keep saying, I see that most ppl didn't necessary wanted to delve too deep in. 
But lets just think about it: everytime the quant metrics, momentum metrics start to signal for a pullback, some announcement happens from gov part. If you wouldn't know the narrative behind, only followed the math metrics, they would have given you a lot of false signals. 
Somebody pointed that out, that in this Trump's chaotic evonironment it was very tough. 
Now, looking back the mechanisms are clear, but when you were in the process? 
Who called the '24s April bottom as target, vol selling, hidden QE, deflationary concerns repricing, China's lie foreshadowing unfold, supportive April OpEx, and stuff...? 
Those were there...
The trade signals were there to help you.

Now, that weighing machine took place and shifted the regime, we can go back to normal, micro.
Macro draws the channel, micro fills the noise in it. 
Again, all i wanted is to show you how these macros are working. While I understand that some ppl were lost in it. 
My basic assumption is always that ppl take the signals, and every grasps of the content I put out... that is not necessary right. 
What should i say..?

comment.body:
I personally can relate to both sides. These markets are challenging to navigate and without the right process and experience these markets will drain your wallet and you emotionally. I agree that some of the questions are very basic and should better be asked to google or ChatGPT than this forum.  But since you, Alma, are promoting this sub very actively on twitter, this has to be accepted and is just a testament of your popularity and appeal to all kinds of investors/traders. So I think they should just be accepted and/or ignored.
I personally also agree that the substack has lost some of its (still extraordinary value) for me, due to the larger focus on geopolitics. I am conducting my own analysis and doing my own research. I am here to refine my edge. That’s why I don’t even check the trades because I don’t care about the signals. But since I’m here to become even better, that implies by definition adapting or overruling some of my own systems/processes when I think the current times might be not accurately reflected in my historical dataset/backtest. So I ended up overrruling my own systems to some extent because I placed higher value on some of your statements in the beginning of April than on my own process. This discretionary decision lead to some losses on my side and missing some of the upside. I don’t blame you, for that because it is my own responsibility. But I get that people might be frustrated, even the ones that are not just waiting for up or down advice. Because exactly the fact that I did not follow your trades but took your context analysis as a factor in my decision making lead for me personally to a worse situation as if I would have just blindly followed your trading.
My problem with all the macro stuff is that it is by definition irrelevant to my process (I am aware about the contradiction…) as long as it is based on insider sources that I cannot access. I don’t know who these insiders are, what is their motivation to share with you and what exactly they share with you. I don’t know if the information is accurate and I don’t know if your conclusion about the information shared is logical. This is not meant as criticism but just as my approach to assessing the information you provide. It did not help that some of the information you seem to have received from your (Russian) insider read like straight propaganda or in fact was propaganda that could have easily fact checked even then (for example the allegedly 1000 encircled Ukrainian soldiers in Kursk). So because of these facts I personally discount a lot of this information. That being said a lot of your calls were accurate,so I am not discrediting per se. But it exemplifies the challenge to filter the signals from the noise. The same applies to some extent to your market calls(short vol etc.). It introduces a bias without telling on what analysis/framework/indicator that bias is based on. For somebody eager to learn and improve that makes it hard to use these because I either can blindly follow (which I don’t want to) or just ignore this information because I have no way of reproducing it systematically. That’s why I think your current impulse to prove your trading prowess or the accuracy of your calls is also misguided because it contradicts your own stated premise for this substack of people thinking for themselves and learning how to trade under your guidance. Blindly following your trades is the opposite of that, even if they where absolutely spot on.
And as has been said before, once you leave (if you still leave), none of this will stay with us and therefore is hardly relevant for people who are building long term systems to trade and invest in the markets. For this to be the case, a lot more of the framework would need to be laid out and the line separating own deduction from inside info would need to be even clearer. And even if we leave the geopolitics out and just focus on the economic stuff in your daily analysis it is sometimes hard to understand why you show certain things on a specific day. Your daily and weekly analysis reads much more professional in tone and refined in grammar/spelling than in the beginning but sometimes it feels like some numbers/charts are just included to fill the blank because the relevance to the given day or the general trading environment is sometimes not obvious.
One thing I am personally disappointed about is that there is no engagement from your side with the more technical/coding related questions. Me and others have asked questions about model parametrizations/implementations but rarely have received an answer.
But after all the criticism I want to end this on a positive note. You provide an incredible amount of value and I am still a happy subscriber. I am thankful for what you do and the service you provide to us. I can hardly fathom the pressure you must feel to perform every day and to try to navigate these markets for thousands of readers. So please take my criticism under that premise.

In some of my educational posts I dropped that sentiment is also monitored, very closely by quant desks. Ppl don't like to talk about it, but it is being done. 
And this is why I thought to share the processes, describing the intention behind US, and its POV (not taking any party personally), bcs that will dictate the decisions. And events are unfolding. 

But, as I said, and keep saying, I see that most ppl didn't necessary wanted to delve too deep in. 
But lets just think about it: everytime the quant metrics, momentum metrics start to signal for a pullback, some announcement happens from gov part. If you wouldn't know the narrative behind, only followed the math metrics, they would have given you a lot of false signals. 
Somebody pointed that out, that in this Trump's chaotic evonironment it was very tough. 
Now, looking back the mechanisms are clear, but when you were in the process? 
Who called the '24s April bottom as target, vol selling, hidden QE, deflationary concerns repricing, China's lie foreshadowing unfold, supportive April OpEx, and stuff...? 
Those were there...
The trade signals were there to help you.

Now, that weighing machine took place and shifted the regime, we can go back to normal, micro.
Macro draws the channel, micro fills the noise in it. 
Again, all i wanted is to show you how these macros are working. While I understand that some ppl were lost in it. 
My basic assumption is always that ppl take the signals, and every grasps of the content I put out... that is not necessary right. 
What should i say..?

comment.body:
I honestly didn’t expect my comment to spark so much response, but I see the feedback and open exchange as something very positive. I think it’s a clear sign that the chat is active and engaged - and that your analyses are respected by everyone here.
Alma, I fully agree with you: if someone doesn’t understand the information being shared, they absolutely should not base trades on it. And let me clearly emphasize that I don’t believe you're ever responsible for anyone’s individual decisions - each member has to assess their own trades and decide whether they make sense for their strategy or not.
I’ve never just scrolled past your posts or chat notes - quite the opposite, actually. I’ve filled an entire notebook with insights and observations. I use my own system and signals and try to integrate your information where it fits. That’s how it should be - because I think copy trading alone won’t make anyone successful. My goal is to refine my own system, question my indicators, and grow as a trader. That’s what made this sub so valuable in the first place.
I also want to fully echo what Chris wrote in his feedback: my trading can’t be built on politics, geopolitical narratives, or potential insider information - simply because I will never have access to the same information you might have, and therefore, I can’t improve my own edge that way. 
Lastly, I also want to end on a very positive note: none of what I said was ever meant as an attack - on the contrary, I deeply respect the work you put in, your commitment, and your analysis. I’m truly thankful for it, however I really had a hard time to filter the important signals from all your analyses.

comment.body:
Thank you Alma.  This is my first time chat.  Not only do I greatly appreciate your daily/weekly write ups... but maybe even more so I appreciate your "ATTENTION" Chat stream-of-conscious Smoke Breaks 🚬🙂.  It demonstrates thought process.  It connect dots for me from analysis as market unfolds in real time. It reiterates the importance of a trading plan and journaling (for every individual, to not get lost in new information / let bias take over decision making).  It reflects importance of time horizons and events and different outcomes.

For anyone who had a bad month, do not give up. Please reflect and be open to idea of how to improve, where did I miss something?  What time horizon? What size did I trade? What am I okay accepting as loss if trade is wrong? Am I hedged? It sucks to have a loss but if you want to get better at the never ending game... it is critical to let loss go and remain balanced mentally.  The mental is real... sell winners too early and hold losers too long bc of the mental. 

I just want to point out something that helps me remain mentally neutral... is have different positions, at different entires with different time horizons that can be short and long.  Using spreads to define risk/reward.  Like many of you, I wanted some form of exposure to a fall off a cliff potential path...

The tell for me Alma trade was the short VIX...  That was a time horizon for many weeks. Within that, there were also many short scalps with time horizon of intraday, 1 day.  That context should speak to you.  If it doesn't, go back and reflect - what was she doing, what do those actions + analysis say to you in hindsight?  Maybe we wouldn't being having this conversation if at the time, Alma said "580 May 16 call" but that is also a lot of risk for that to go to zero!! She gave a great core trade with time horizon to help navigate... and obviously, options were expense but hopefully you get the point.  

Much love ❤️🙏🏼🚬
